name: Lemon Juice / Vitamin C

book_claims.claim: Increases iron and zinc absorption from plant foods
book_claims.mechanism: Vitamin C reduces ferric iron (Fe3+) to ferrous iron (Fe2+) which is more readily absorbed; also chelates minerals making them more bioavailable

supplementary_research.finding: Hallberg et al. (1989) demonstrated that 50mg of ascorbic acid (roughly one lemon's juice) increased non-heme iron absorption by 3-6x in controlled meals, with the effect strongest when consumed simultaneously with the iron source.

Carr & Maggini (2017) reviewed vitamin C's role in immune function, finding it supports epithelial barrier function, enhances neutrophil and lymphocyte activity, and that deficiency significantly impairs immunity. 100-200mg/day optimal for infection prevention.

Green et al. (2013) showed that adding citrus juice to green tea stabilised catechins in the alkaline gut environment, potentially increasing EGCG bioavailability by up to 6x, suggesting vitamin C-rich foods enhance other protective compounds.

culinary_pairings.source: Dramatically improves iron absorption; standard seasoning for dal and bean soups
Vitamin C enhances iron and zinc uptake from spinach, kale, chard
Increases mineral bioavailability from grain-based dishes
Stabilises catechins, improving EGCG absorption up to 6x
Classic vinaigrette base; combined antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects
Acid + allicin both enhance mineral absorption; foundational flavour combination
Traditional preparation; acidity brightens and balances rich proteins

seasonality: Lemons available year-round from multiple growing regions. Peak Northern Hemisphere season November-April. Store at room temperature for juicing (more juice when warm), refrigerate for longer storage. Juice freezes well in ice cube trays.

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Nearly 100% bioavailable at dietary doses. One lemon's juice (~30ml) provides ~12mg vitamin C. Degrades rapidly with heat and exposure to air.
Chelates minerals to improve absorption. Also creates acidic environment that favours iron conversion.
Concentrated in zest. Promotes phase II detoxification enzymes. Anti-cancer properties in animal models.
Primary lemon flavonoid. Antioxidant and anti-diabetic properties in preclinical studies.
Modest contribution per serving but adds up with daily use.

synergies.mechanism: Vitamin C increases non-heme iron absorption 2-6x by reducing Fe3+ to Fe2+
Vitamin C stabilises catechins in alkaline gut, improving EGCG absorption up to 6x
Acidic environment may improve curcumin solubility alongside piperine
